china.descriptor: China-linked donor tracking. The CCP operates an explicit United Front Work Department to direct overseas members and proxies — including business executives such as Wanxiang Group's Pin Ni (named CCP member 15 times in Chinese state media) — to cultivate political relationships in the United States. Federal law prohibits CCP members from donating to U.S. campaigns, and most CCP-member donors hold green cards rather than citizenship, raising significant legal questions. RESOLUTE Citizen treats any documented contribution from a CCP-member or United Front-affiliated donor as a failure on america_first/q3.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Strong conservative HARD record, no deviations: pro-life (voted NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment SJ247/HJR1, on-record parental-consent objection; ReproRising 2025 grade F; sponsored SB163 barring abortion-requiring surrogacy clauses); held the marriage line (voted NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment with explicit faith rationale — 'my faith teaches me marriage is between one man and one woman' — NOT a crossover, unlike Durant); strong parental-rights advocacy; CHIEF PATRON of voter-photo-ID and registration-verification bills (SB168, SB1070); pro-2A (on record: the assault-weapons ban is 'a direct infringement on the Second Amendment'). NOTE: he resigned the RPV CHAIRMANSHIP 12/31/2025 but remains the sitting SD-8 senator. Economic-stewardship, public-justice, federal-overreach, border, and christian-liberty cells left NULL — no itemized Peake-specific record found (VPAP donor page 403). Sourcing note: lis.virginia.gov JS-rendered; named votes corroborated via VPM, Virginia Mercury, Ballotpedia News, ReproRising PDF, Wikipedia, official Senate member page.

candidates.name: Tammy Brankley Mulchi
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-9 (sitting since Jan 2024 special election). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent conservative HARD record (richest roll call of this batch, via Richmond Sunlight mirror of the official feed): pro-life (voted NO on SJ247/HJ1 2025 and HJ1/SJ1 2026 reproductive-freedom amendments, plus SB754, SB743; ReproRising 2025 grade F); held the marriage line (voted NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment in BOTH 2025 and 2026 — not among the GOP crossovers); affirms biological sex (chief patron of SB723/SB749 women's-sports bills and SJ49 'affirming biological differences between the sexes'); tough-on-crime victim/penalty sponsorships (SB673 stalking, SB700 repeat prostitution-solicitation penalties, SB616/SB653 protective orders, SB844 sex-offender registry) plus SB1420 (forfeited assets for law-enforcement use); strongly pro-2A (voted NO across the entire 2025-26 gun-control slate — assault-firearms ban SB1181, magazine limits, waiting period, mandatory storage — and YES on the SB1389 safety-device tax credit). Election-integrity left NULL: she cast many votes on Democratic-majority election bills but the per-bill direction could not be verified, so no integrity signal is scored (honest null). Christian-liberty, federal-overreach, border, family-choice, and economic CBDC/ESG cells left NULL. Sourcing note: VPAP/new LIS API/legiscan unreachable; roll calls from Richmond Sunlight (Open-Virginia mirror of the official Legislature feed), crossover list from Ballotpedia News.

candidates.name: Creigh Deeds
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-11 (sitting since 2001). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Long-tenured mainstream Senate Democrat. Pro-abortion-rights HARD record: named 2023 'Reproductive Freedom Champion' by ReproRising (formerly NARAL), endorsed by the group, in the Democratic majority advancing the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247/HJ1). Marriage: in the majority advancing the same-sex-marriage amendment (2025 & 2026 references) and publicly opposed Youngkin's transgender-student model policy. Opposed school choice/ESAs ('nonstarter… defund public schools') and voter ID (urged a 2012 veto). Economic: backs the $15 minimum wage. Public justice: sponsored bail reform (SB1266) and voted to abolish the death penalty (2021). Guns: sponsored an assault-weapons ban (SB1382), backed universal background checks/safe-storage; GIFFORDS-endorsed. CROSS-CURRENT (historical, noted not scored): in 2009 voted against in-state tuition/benefits for undocumented immigrants and for English-as-official-language — restrictionist votes atypical of his caucus, but 2009-era and not mapping to the current border cells. Several amendment roll-call lines are party-line-majority inferences (VPAP blocked automated read) but his alignment is independently corroborated by HARD endorsements/statements. No conservative deviations on the scored cells.

profile.background: VA State Senator since 2001 (House of Delegates 1992-2001); attorney, former Bath County Commonwealth's Attorney; 2005 AG nominee and 2009 Democratic gubernatorial nominee. SD-11 is a sprawling central/western VA district (Bath, Alleghany, Charlottesville/Albemarle area) after redistricting from the old SD-25. Virginia's leading mental-health-reform legislator following his son's 2013 death.
profile.education: Concord College (BA, 1980), Wake Forest University School of Law (JD, 1984)

candidates.name: Glen Sturtevant
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-12 (returned to the Senate 2024; prior service 2016-2020). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent Christian-conservative record. Life: self-described '100% pro-life,' graded F by ReproRising 2025, opposed the reproductive-freedom amendment with the GOP bloc. Marriage (VERIFIED, the key cell): voted NO on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026) — the ONLY 5 Republican crossovers were DeSteph, Diggs, Durant, Jordan, and Suetterlein; Sturtevant was in the 13-member NO bloc, did NOT cross. Family: backs school choice ('money follows the child') and sponsored parental content-opt-out legislation. Elections: supports restoring photo voter ID. Immigration: restrictionist; sponsored an ICE-transfer bill for incarcerated persons. Guns: opposed the 2026 mandatory safe-storage bill and argued to repeal the red-flag law. ONE DOCUMENTED RECORD DEVIATION (noted, not scored): voted FOR red-flag legislation in 2019 but now favors repeal — his current position is opposition, which is what's scored. Cells with no public evidence (christian liberty, federal overreach, CBDC/ESG, death-penalty, a numeric Giffords grade) are left null, NOT scored from party.

Served the old SD-10 2016-2020, lost in 2019, returned to the Senate in 2024 after winning the 2023 primary and general. He and his wife are raising four adopted children.

candidates.name: Lamont Bagby
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-14 (sitting since 2023; former Delegate). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniform progressive record (CPAC lifetime rating 12%). Life: voted YEA on SJ247 (reproductive-freedom amendment, named in the 21-Yea roll call). Marriage: in the Democratic bloc advancing the same-sex-marriage amendment (SJ249 2025, HJ3 2026). Economic: party-line YES on the $15 minimum wage; VA League of Conservation Voters-endorsed. Elections: lead figure in the 2020 Democratic election-law overhaul that REPEALED photo voter ID, created no-excuse early voting and same-day/automatic registration; patron of Sunday-voting legislation; voted for the felon-voting-rights amendment. Immigration: voted with the majority for the 2026 package limiting ICE cooperation (SB351/SB352). Guns: GIFFORDS-endorsed (one-handgun-a-month, red-flag, background checks). NO cross-party deviations found. christian_liberty, refuse_federal_overreach, school-choice and public_justice cells had no specific cited vote → left null rather than inferred.

profile.religion: No publicly documented personal church affiliation (has sponsored commending resolutions for several Richmond-area Black congregations, but no source states his own membership)
profile.background: VA State Senator for SD-14 (parts of Henrico County and Richmond City) since a March 2023 special election; former Delegate 2015-2023 and former Henrico County School Board member. Former Henrico High teacher/administrator; chairs the Virginia Legislative Black Caucus and was elected Democratic Party of Virginia chair in 2025.

candidates.name: Michael Jones
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-15 (sitting since Jan 2026; former Delegate HD-77; former Richmond City Council President). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] HONEST KEY FINDING: Jones is a Christian pastor (Village of Faith Ministries), but his LIS-verified recorded votes are uniformly progressive — pastoral identity did NOT produce a socially conservative voting record, and he is scored on his votes, not his vocation. Life: co-patron + YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1/SJ247 2025; HB781/SB449 2026); ReproRising A+. Marriage: YES on HJ9 (2025) repealing the one-man/one-woman definition and YES on HB612/SB311 (2026). Economic: YES on multiple minimum-wage bills and paid family leave. Elections: YES on the felon-voting-rights amendment (HJ2) and on expanding in-person absentee voting/registration. Public justice: introduced bills banning tear gas/rubber bullets/chokeholds and voted for marijuana-offense resentencing. Guns: chief patron of an auto-sears ban (HB22) and YES on the assault-firearms/high-capacity-magazine ban (HB1607); GIFFORDS-endorsed. Across ~2,094 recorded 2025 House votes he cast only 3 idiosyncratic NO votes, none a conservative break. christian_liberty (a church-property tax-exemption YES does not map to the symbol/prayer/conscience cells), refuse_federal_overreach, school choice, and sanctuary cells left null.

profile.religion: Christian; founding Senior Pastor of Village of Faith Ministries (non-denominational, Richmond area). M.Div., Samuel DeWitt Proctor School of Theology (Virginia Union); D.Min., McCormick Theological Seminary
profile.background: VA State Senator for SD-15 (parts of Richmond City and Chesterfield County) since Jan 2026; former Delegate HD-77 (2024-2026) and Richmond City Council member/President (2016-2023). Full-time pastor and Democrat; won the Jan 2026 special election for the seat vacated when Ghazala Hashmi became Lt. Governor.

candidates.name: Schuyler VanValkenburg
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-16 (Henrico); sitting since Jan 2024; term runs through 2028. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. Co-patron + YEA on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247 2025 / HJ1 2026); ReproRising 2025 scorecard A+ ('Champion'); ChoiceTracker 'Pro-Choice' (Planned Parenthood Action endorsed). YEA on the same-sex-marriage amendment HJ3 (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026). Vocal opponent of Education Savings Accounts / school choice. Lead sponsor of legislation returning VA to the ERIC multistate voter-data partnership; in the Democratic majority that advanced felon-voting restoration (HJ2). In the Senate Democratic bloc that passed the 2026 ICE-cooperation restrictions (21-19 party line) and the $15 minimum-wage law; championed the 2026 gun-reform package (assault-weapons ban, magazine limit, red-flag expansion). No conservative deviations found. RECORD CORRECTION: SB1031 (homeschool religious-exemption repeal) belongs to Sen. Pekarsky, NOT VanValkenburg — do not attribute it to him.

profile.religion: Presbyterian (per VA Senate member page / LegiStorm; not emphasized publicly)
profile.background: VA State Senator, SD-16 (Henrico County), sitting since Jan 2024; defeated incumbent Siobhan Dunnavant Nov 2023; former Delegate HD-72 (2018-2024). History/U.S. Government teacher at Glen Allen HS, National Board Certified; the only public-school teacher in the Senate. B.A. History (Univ. of Richmond), M.A. History (VCU); father of three.

candidates.name: Emily Jordan
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-17, sitting since Jan 2024 (former Delegate HD-64). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Genuinely conservative HARD record (Family Foundation report card 95% pro-family, 18 of 19 votes) with ONE notable deviation. KEY DEVIATION (verified, LIS roll call): voted YES on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026), one of ~5 Republicans crossing over — biblical_marriage q0/q1 FALSE. On the same day she held the conservative line on the other two amendments: NO on HJ1 (abortion) and NO on HJ2 (felon voting), so the marriage vote is a deliberate, isolated break, not a moderate drift. Pro-life voting (NO HJ1); pro-parental-notification (NO on SB19/HB1499 narrowing the explicit-material notice law; YES SB817; YES SB778 obscene-material-to-minor penalty); YES HB131 (religious accommodations in higher ed); fiscal-conservative (NO on the $15 minimum wage SB1); election-integrity (NO on expanded Sunday absentee SB438, NO on felon restoration); pro-police (NO on SB352 restricting LEO, YES SB55/SB111 penalties, NO on SB60 parole expansion); anti-sanctuary (NO on SB783/HB1441 ICE-restriction bills); strongly pro-2A (NO on assault-weapons ban SB749, red-flag SB495, storage mandate SB348).

profile.background: VA State Senator, SD-17, sitting since Jan 2024; former Delegate HD-64 (2018-2024). Small-business owner (wine shop/restaurant), first-responder trainer; Windsor Ruritan. Undergrad Regent University. Married (née Brewer).
profile.birthplace: Wyandot County, Ohio (raised in Suffolk, Virginia)
profile.education: Regent University

candidates.name: L. Louise Lucas
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-18; Senate President pro tempore; Chair, Finance & Appropriations; in the GA since 1992. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive and unusually activist HARD record: CHIEF PATRON of all three 2026 constitutional amendments — reproductive freedom (SJR1), same-sex marriage (SJR3, repealing the Marshall-Newman ban), and felon-voting restoration (SJR2). ReproRising 2025 scorecard A+ ('Champion'). VCDL 2025 record 0% pro-gun; chief patron of the 2026 assault-weapons ban SB749; Giffords 'champion.' Chief patron of SB1 ($15 minimum wage) plus paid-sick-leave (SB199), paid family leave (SB2), child-care assistance (SB3). As Finance Chair, killed Youngkin's private-school voucher proposal ('not going to happen') and declared his sanctuary-funding-restriction budget item dead. Chief patron of SB438 (expanded Sunday early voting) and SB322 (National Popular Vote compact). No conservative deviations.

profile.religion: Baptist (Deaconess, New Mount Olivet Baptist Church); some structured sources list African Methodist Episcopal — denomination conflict noted
profile.background: VA State Senator, SD-18 (Portsmouth/Chesapeake); in the General Assembly since 1992; Senate President pro tempore and Chair of Finance & Appropriations. First female shipfitter at Norfolk Naval Shipyard (1971); first woman and first African American elected Senate Pro Tem. B.S. and M.A., Norfolk State University.

candidates.name: Stella Pekarsky
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-36 (Fairfax); sitting since Jan 2024; former Fairfax County School Board chair. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Reliably progressive record. SANCTITY OF LIFE: patron of and voted YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247 SV89); ReproRising 'A+'; ChoiceTracker 'Pro-Choice'; sponsored SB137 (2026, clinic-protection); on record '100% pro-choice'; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA + EMILY's List. MARRIAGE/GENDER: in the Democratic bloc on the same-sex-marriage amendment (SJ249); voted YES on SB1098 (Reproductive & Gender-Affirming Health Care Act); OPPOSED 'Sage's Law' (parental notification on gender transition) and moved to reject Youngkin's gender-privacy budget amendment. FAMILY/CHILD: her opposition to parental-notification-on-gender (Sage's Law) is a documented contrary position on parental sovereignty. ECONOMICS: party-line support for the $15 minimum wage (SB1). IMMIGRATION: co-patron of SB491 (bars schools disclosing student data to immigration) and SB351 (limits ICE in 'protected areas'). SELF-DEFENSE: Giffords-endorsed; sponsored SB115 (2026, restrict out-of-state CCW reciprocity) and SB255 (2024, safe-storage notification). Christian-liberty (SB1031 ambiguous), federal-overreach, election (voter-ID/mail-in), and police/bail public-justice cells = NO clean public record, left NULL.

Ran for U.S. House (VA-11) in the 2025 primary and lost; retains her state Senate seat.

candidates.name: Chris Yakabouski
candidates.notes: Longest-serving BOS member (since 2003). Self-identifies as far from moderate. Won 2025 re-election by only 16 votes vs. Braswell. Has clashed with local tea party/Patriots group over stadium and taxes. Runs Bob Yak and Sons Home Remodeling. Strong on 2A but has voted for tax increases, which draws conservative criticism. Pragmatic Republican — not a culture warrior.

profile.background: Conservative Spotsylvania supervisor, Battlefield District

claims.text: Strong on 2A but has voted for tax increases, which draws conservative criticism.

candidates.name: David Goosman
candidates.notes: STRONGEST CHRISTIAN CONSERVATIVE on the board. Baptist deacon who explicitly campaigns on Christian conservative principles. Retired Air Force officer, 21 years military service. Campaign website is goosmangop.org — the 'GOP' is in the URL. Defeated incumbent Kevin Marshall by a wide margin running as the more conservative option. His organization is explicitly 'committed to advancing Christian conservative leadership in Spotsylvania County.' Newly elected — limited voting record but strong faith-based platform.

candidates.name: Gerald Childress
candidates.notes: Marine veteran and Spotsylvania County native. Graduated from Spotsylvania High School. Served on Planning Commission (Chairman), Transportation Committee, and Economic Development Authority before BOS. Explicitly states belief in 'limited government, individual responsibility, and fiscal conservatism.' Initially pro-data-center development but shifted to support SUP requirement. First term — building voting record.

candidates.name: Drew Mullins
candidates.office: Board of Supervisors — Courtland District (Vice Chairman)
candidates.notes: Most decorated military veteran on the board — 23-year Navy SEAL with 14+ deployments. Elected Vice Chairman in Jan 2026 (Frazier was perturbed by the vote). Campaign focuses on economic development, competitive county employee salaries, and public safety — NOT culture war issues. Pragmatic Republican who emphasizes 'healthy county growth.' Small business owner (Azimuth Analytics). UMW graduate pursuing graduate degree at University of Arizona.

candidates.name: Lori Hayes
candidates.office: Board of Supervisors — Lee Hill District (Chairman)
candidates.notes: Board Chairman and independent who caucuses with Republicans. Social work degree from GMU. Co-owned Calypso Kennels in Stafford with her brother (who died in 2023). Strongest 2A score due to proactive leadership on sanctuary reaffirmation. Runs as independent — NOT endorsed through GOP nomination. Supported redistricting referendum opposition (6-0). Pragmatic, growth-management focused. Limited public positions on social/cultural issues — scores lower on life, marriage, and christian_nationalism due to absence of evidence, not contrary evidence.

profile.background: Chairman, Spotsylvania Board of Supervisors, Lee Hill District

candidates.name: Jacob Lane
candidates.notes: Blue-collar Republican — Luck Stone foreman who has been involved in county issues since his 20s. Won Feb 2022 special election after Barry Jett's death. Claims ideological alignment with Jett. Vocally defended the board's redistricting referendum resolution saying it was about 'equal and fair' timing for both sides. One of the most vocal 2A supporters — brought it up proactively during the Jan 2026 meeting. Youngest BOS member at ~40. Infrastructure and growth management focused.

candidates.name: Dr. Deborah H. Frazier
candidates.notes: LONE DEMOCRAT on the board. First African American BOS member in Spotsylvania history. Career educator (25-year principal, now Chief Academic Officer). Abstained from collective bargaining opposition letter (6-0, she abstained) saying she was 'on the fence.' Abstained from redistricting referendum opposition (6-0, she abstained). Was 'perturbed' when Mullins was elected Vice Chair over her or her preferred candidate in Jan 2026. Served on Spanberger's gubernatorial transition team. Notable: she voted YES on 2A sanctuary BOTH times — suggesting some independence from Democratic Party on gun issues in this rural-suburban county. Scored 2 on self_defense because she did support 2A sanctuary. Education score reflects career dedication but from progressive establishment perspective, not parental rights/school choice angle.

claims.text: Notable: she voted YES on 2A sanctuary BOTH times — suggesting some independence from Democratic Party on gun issues in this rural-suburban county.

candidates.name: Kerry Devine
candidates.notes:
Mayor since 2024. Registered Independent but Dem-endorsed. Council under her leadership: LGBTQIA+ Pride Month, DEI commitment, 100% renewable energy resolution. No public stances on abortion, marriage, immigration, or religious liberty. Supports gun giveback program.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor, At-Large, term 2024-2027 (fredericksburgva.gov/263). HARD evidence: voted for FY2027 budget raising the real-estate tax rate 4 cents to $0.84/$100 and a two-tier water/sewer rate hike, and personally pushed to advertise a rate 1 cent higher 'for flexibility' (economic_stewardship FALSE). Publicly supported the city Gun Give-Back and backed Gov. Northam's proposal giving localities more authority to regulate firearms (self_defense FALSE). As mayor, the city officially observes LGBTQIA+ Pride Month, flies Pride flags downtown, and maintains a Commitment to Equity & Inclusion (biblical_marriage q4 FALSE, institutional). The FY2027 budget she presided over funds a public-safety pay plan with no defunding — counted as routine governance, not the affirmative public advocacy public_justice q0 requires, so left null. No church/faith affiliation found in two long-form interviews or her official bio. NO EVIDENCE found on abortion, schools curriculum, election administration, or immigration at the council level — those cells are null, not penalized.

profile.education: University of Mary Washington
profile.background: Social studies teacher, Stafford County. City Council since 2004. School Board 1996-2004. Mother of four.

candidates.name: Charlie Frye Jr.
candidates.notes:
Most documented progressive on council. Teamster (Local 322), 24-yr UPS driver. Created gun giveback program (200+ guns collected since 2014). Led slave auction block removal. 100% renewable energy by 2035. Progressive Voters Guide "most progressive choice." Member Shiloh Baptist Church but policy positions entirely progressive.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Vice Mayor, Ward 4, re-elected/named VM Jan 2026, term 2026-2029. HARD evidence: founded the Fredericksburg Gun Give-Back in 2014 (242 firearms collected by Jan 2026), championed statewide HB702 modeled on it, and backed expanding local firearm-regulation authority (self_defense FALSE). Voted for two consecutive 4-cent real-estate tax-rate increases — FY2026 to $0.80 and FY2027 to $0.84/$100 (economic_stewardship FALSE). Public safety his stated No. 1 priority; runs police-partnership programs ('Murder Free Fredericksburg') and credited the city with zero homicides in 2025 (public_justice TRUE). Prior speculative party-default FALSEs on abortion/marriage/elections cleared to null — NO direct evidence of personal positions on those was found.

profile.background: Vice Mayor since 2020. Ward 4 Councilman since 2014. Only Black member of council. Gun give-back program. Racial equity advocate.

candidates.notes:
Attorney and retired archaeologist. NAACP executive board member and Legal Redress Committee. Focuses on workforce development, historic preservation. Avoids single-issue politics. Won Ward 2 with 60%. Almost no culture-war positions documented. Resigned/replaced 2025; seat now held by Joy Y. Crump.

candidates.name: Joshua Cole
candidates.notes: VA Delegate, HD-65 (Fredericksburg area); Baptist pastor; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record and an active LEADER of the measures most opposed by this rubric: CHIEF SPONSOR of the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJR1; A+/'Champion' from ReproRising) and CHIEF SPONSOR of the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3; he is himself bisexual). Led automatic voter registration + felon-voting-rights restoration. Gun-control endorsed (Giffords: voted for safe-storage + universal background checks). Supported pro-transgender school policy; opposes book 'bans.' No conservative deviations found. Confirms the prior 0/70 read. (Note: this record was de-duplicated 2026-06 — a second 'joshua-g-cole' HD-65 record was merged in.)

candidates.name: Libby Humphries
candidates.notes: Commonwealth's Attorney, City of Fredericksburg (elected chief prosecutor since 2021; re-elected Nov 2025). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence — self-described reform/progressive prosecutor: runs an explicit cashless (risk-based) bail policy (public_justice q1 FALSE); frames criminal behavior 'from a public health standpoint' and prioritizes 'community safety and health over punishment' with expanded diversion/alternatives to incarceration (public_justice q3, q4 FALSE); supports Fair and Just Prosecution (a national progressive-prosecutor network) and the PD's '8 Can't Wait' reforms; named 'the progressive choice' by the Progressive Voters Guide in 2021 and 2025. Counter-evidence (mixed, so q2 left null not FALSE): she retains victim-services staff and has a real violent-crime conviction record, including six life terms against a child abuser and multiple murder convictions. NO blanket declination-to-prosecute policy found. NO EVIDENCE on marriage/Christian-liberty/elections/state-overreach/immigration — null.

profile.background: Commonwealth's Attorney for the City of Fredericksburg since Aug 2021 (re-elected Nov 2025, 66%-32%). Career prosecutor 21+ yrs (Charlottesville CA 1999-2018; Fredericksburg Deputy CA 2018-2021). JD University of Virginia, BA Dartmouth.

r1.title: Explicit cashless bail: 'An exclusively risk-based bail policy is a cashless bail policy'; supports Fair and Just Prosecution (progressive-prosecutor network).

r2.excerpt: Reform prosecutor focused on decarceration and alternatives to incarceration; does not affirm a retributive 'bear the sword' posture.

profile.background: U.S. Marine veteran (enlisted 2002 after 9/11). Prosecutor with 10+ years trial experience. Father of four.

candidates.notes: Ward 1 council member (term 2026-2029); Democratic-endorsed (Fredericksburg Democratic Committee, Nov 2025). Notably, he was the LONE 6-1 dissenter against the FY2027 two-tier water/sewer rate structure — but his stated reason was renter/affordability EQUITY (apartment residents paying more per gallon), not tax restraint, and he still supported the headline tax increase. As school-board chair he authored a professional-development spending-control policy (fiscal-governance positive, school-board tier). 2016 (VA-01 race): supported comprehensive immigration reform, legal status for undocumented workers, and protecting DREAMers (documented but a federal-race position; no local ICE vote, so border cells left null). NO EVIDENCE on life/marriage/family/Christian-liberty/elections at the council level — those cells null.
